summary: A clean soundboard capture of an electrifying early‑80s Hult Center show, highlighted by a blazing “Eyes of the World”, soaring China > Rider jam and a fiery “Other One”.
review: The recording is a solid soundboard source that sounds clear and loud for most of the set, with only a brief dip in quality at the start of “Eyes”. The performance is consistently high‑energy – the band runs at breakneck speed, delivering an incendiary “Eyes”, a monster China > Rider sequence and a standout “The Other One”, while Jerry’s solo work, Phil’s aggressive bass and Bob’s crisp rhythm draw frequent praise.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ≈ 4.2 combined with overwhelmingly positive comments about the show; recording quality is good overall despite a few minor mix complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clean, loud and clear; a brief “B‑grade” dip at the beginning of “Eyes” noted by one reviewer, and a few comments about the mix being less balanced (Phil’s bass prominent).

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑octane, fast‑paced set that feels tight and intimate; the band rides a wave of energy throughout, with seamless transitions and an especially fiery second half.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for shredding on “Eyes”, delivering the fastest solo many have heard and keeping pace with the rest of the band.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for a more aggressive, prominent bass line that adds drive to the performance; some reviewers mention his mix prominence.
band_member_comments.Bob: His rhythm work described as loud, clear and interesting throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill & Mickey praised for keeping up with the band’s lightning speed.
